pages.raw_text:
Unusual storms coming on top OF
stringency in the coal market
have overtaxed even Spokane's
Transportation facilities, and If there
is any coal on hand spyglass
would be needed to find it today.


For dealers who have been ship
ping, or trying to ship, over the
Great Northern and Northern Pa
cific, the outlook is dark and chilly.
What coal is moved is promptly
confscated by the companies.


The coal situation has been
brought to an acute stage here by
the blocking of the Spokane & In
ternational line to British Columbia
by the storms of the past week,
With that road tied up even for a
very short while the city is getting
taste Of what 1t would have to
undergo indefinitely, perhaps, If it
depended on the other lines. For
tunately there is plenty of wood in
sight and more of it on the way.


So far no raise in prices have
been reported except in the case OF
the Consumers Fuel & Ice Co.,
